name: Crossword
description: Spell the orthopaedic terms matching the clues.

challenges.clue: Longest and strongest bone in the human body, located in the thigh
Bone forming the heel
S-shaped bone connecting the sternum to the scapula
Triangular flat bone at the back of the shoulder, also called the shoulder blade
Bone of the upper arm, between shoulder and elbow
Medial bone of the forearm, in line with the little finger
Lateral bone of the forearm, in line with the thumb
Weight-bearing bone of the lower leg, also called the shin bone
Thin outer bone of the lower leg that does not bear much body weight
Small triangular bone embedded in the quadriceps tendon at the front of the knee
Cup-shaped socket of the hip bone that receives the head of the femur
Bony prominence on the posterior forearm that forms the point of the elbow
Ring of eight small carpal bones connecting the hand to the forearm
Five long bones of the palm, between the carpals and the fingers
Bones of the fingers and toes
Flat, curved bones that form the protective cage around the heart and lungs
Flat bone in the center of the chest where the ribs attach via cartilage
Backbone made up of 33 stacked bones from the neck to the tailbone
Dome-shaped bone forming the roof of the skull
Only movable bone of the skull, forming the lower jaw
Bony ridge on the shoulder blade that you can feel at the tip of the shoulder
Hook-like projection of the scapula that curves toward the front of the shoulder
Shallow socket on the scapula that articulates with the head of the humerus
Rounded bony bump at the end of a bone near a joint, such as at the elbow or knee
Ankle bone that sits atop the calcaneus and articulates with the tibia and fibula
Bony bump on the inner or outer side of the ankle, formed by the tibia or fibula
Boat-shaped tarsal bone on the medial side of the foot, between the talus and cuneiforms
Bony process felt on the greater trochanter side of the hip where gluteal muscles attach
Rough projection on a bone where a tendon or ligament firmly attaches, e.g. the tibial one below the knee
Rounded bump above a bone's condyle where ligaments attach, found on the femur and humerus
Thin membrane lining the inner surface of a joint capsule that secretes lubricating fluid
Smooth connective tissue covering the ends of bones inside a joint, reducing friction during movement
C-shaped fibrocartilage disc that cushions and stabilizes the knee joint between femur and tibia
Fibrous band of tissue connecting bone to bone, providing joint stability
Tough cord of fibrous tissue connecting muscle to bone
Small fluid-filled sac that reduces friction between tendon, bone, and skin near a joint
Ring of fibrocartilage that deepens a shallow socket joint, as seen in the shoulder and hip
Fibrous sac enclosing a synovial joint and its cavity
General term for the point where two or more bones meet, forming a joint
Freely movable joint type that allows a wide range of motion, such as the shoulder or knee
Immovable joint type where bones are firmly united, such as the sutures of the skull
Slightly movable joint type, such as the joints between vertebral bodies
Cartilaginous joint where bones are joined by fibrocartilage, as seen at the pubic bones
Immovable fibrous joint found between the flat bones of the skull
Fibrous joint where bones are bound by a sheet of connective tissue, as between the tibia and fibula
Tough fibrous membrane covering the outer surface of a bone, rich in nerve and blood supply
Thin membrane lining the inner cavity of a bone, facing the marrow
Long shaft-like middle section of a long bone, between the two ends
Rounded end of a long bone, typically the site of joint formation and growth plate closure
Flared region of a long bone between the shaft and the end, containing the growth plate in children
Bone-forming cell responsible for laying down new bone matrix
Large multinucleated cell that breaks down and resorbs old bone tissue
Mature bone cell embedded within the bone matrix that maintains bone tissue
Mature cartilage-producing cell found within the lacunae of cartilage tissue
Spongy, porous inner bone tissue found at the ends of long bones and inside flat bones
Dense, compact outer layer of bone that gives long bones their strength
Microscopic canal running through compact bone that carries blood vessels and nerves
Smooth, glassy type of cartilage found on articular joint surfaces and the trachea
Tough cartilage rich in collagen fibers, found in the intervertebral discs and menisci
Fibrous joint that anchors a tooth into its socket in the jawbone
Type of ligament that crosses another within a joint cavity, e.g. the anterior and posterior ones stabilizing the knee
Ligament running along the side of a joint (medial or lateral) that resists sideways bending stress
Ligament continuing from the quadriceps tendon, connecting the patella to the tibial tuberosity
Ligament spanning from the coracoid process to the acromion, forming an arch over the shoulder joint
Strong ligament connecting the coracoid process of the scapula to the clavicle
Ligament reinforcing the joint between the acromion and the outer end of the clavicle
Strongest ligament of the hip, running from ilium to femur and preventing over-extension
Hip ligament running from the pubic bone to the femur, resisting excessive abduction
Hip ligament arising from the ischium that spirals around the femoral neck
Ankle ligament connecting the talus to the fibula, most commonly sprained in an inversion injury
Ankle ligament running from the calcaneus to the fibula, part of the lateral ankle ligament complex
Thick band of connective tissue on the sole of the foot supporting the arch, often inflamed in fasciitis
Triangular muscle capping the shoulder that abducts the arm, a common site for intramuscular injections
Large flat muscle of the upper back and neck involved in shrugging and rotating the scapula
Broad, flat muscle of the back nicknamed the 'lat', a strong extensor and adductor of the arm
Rotator cuff muscle running along the top of the scapula that initiates arm abduction
Rotator cuff muscle below the scapular spine that externally rotates the shoulder
Rotator cuff muscle on the front surface of the scapula that internally rotates the shoulder
Two-headed muscle on the front of the upper arm that flexes the elbow and supinates the forearm
Three-headed muscle on the back of the upper arm responsible for elbow extension
Muscle lying deep to the biceps that is the primary flexor of the elbow joint
Four-headed muscle group on the front of the thigh that extends the knee
Group of posterior thigh muscles that flex the knee and extend the hip
Calf muscle with two heads that forms the bulk of the calf and plantarflexes the ankle
Flat calf muscle lying deep to the gastrocnemius, important for standing posture
Longest muscle in the human body, running obliquely across the thigh from hip to knee
Thin, strap-like muscle on the inner thigh that adducts the leg and flexes the knee
Group of three buttock muscles responsible for hip extension and abduction
Small hip muscle deep to gluteus maximus that externally rotates the thigh, near the sciatic nerve
Combined hip flexor muscle formed by the psoas major and iliacus
